questions.question: What is a political party?

options.B: A group of people who contest elections to hold power

questions.explanation: A political party is a group of people who come together to contest elections and hold power in the government.

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of a political party?
questions.explanation: Political parties consist of leaders, active members, and followers.

questions.question: What do political parties do during elections?
questions.explanation: Political parties select candidates, contest elections, and campaign for votes.

questions.question: Which body recognizes political parties in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India registers and recognizes political parties.

questions.question: Which system allows many political parties to compete in elections?
questions.explanation: In a multiparty system, many political parties compete for power.

questions.question: Which country follows a one-party system?
questions.explanation: China is an example of a country with a one-party system.

questions.question: Which political system is followed in India?
questions.explanation: India follows a multiparty system.

questions.question: What is the role of opposition parties?
questions.explanation: Opposition parties criticize government policies and offer alternatives.

questions.question: Which of the following is a national party in India?
questions.explanation: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is a national political party.

questions.question: What do political parties present during elections?
questions.explanation: Political parties present policies and programmes for the public.

questions.question: Which group forms the government after winning the majority of seats?
questions.explanation: The political party that wins the majority forms the government.

questions.question: What do political parties help shape in society?
questions.explanation: Political parties raise issues and influence public opinion.

questions.question: Which of the following represents regional interests?
questions.explanation: State parties focus on regional interests.

questions.question: Which challenge occurs when leadership passes within families?
questions.explanation: Dynastic succession refers to leadership being inherited by family members.

questions.question: Which challenge refers to lack of participation of ordinary members?
questions.explanation: Many political parties lack internal democracy.

questions.question: Which law prevents elected representatives from changing parties?
questions.explanation: The anti-defection law prevents representatives from switching parties after election.

questions.question: Political parties mainly aim to gain what?
questions.explanation: Political parties contest elections to gain political power.

questions.question: Which institution conducts elections in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission conducts elections in India.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of multiparty systems?
questions.explanation: Multiparty systems represent diverse interests.

questions.question: What type of government is formed when several parties join together?
questions.explanation: Coalition governments are formed by alliances of parties.

questions.question: Political parties connect citizens with which institution?
questions.explanation: Political parties connect citizens with the government.

questions.question: Which challenge involves the influence of money during elections?
questions.explanation: Money power can influence candidate selection and election outcomes.

questions.question: Which political party type focuses mainly on state issues?
questions.explanation: State parties focus mainly on regional issues.

questions.question: Which of the following helps improve political parties?
questions.explanation: Political reforms help improve the functioning of political parties.

questions.question: Political parties help citizens by providing access to what?
questions.explanation: Political parties help citizens communicate with government institutions.
